description: Comprehensive eval of all common Jira operations — measuring tool efficiency

evals.name: read-issue
evals.prompt: What's the status and assignee of TEST-135?
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-issue.py get TEST-135 and reports status + assignee

evals.name: search-issues
evals.prompt: Find all open issues in the TEST project assigned to me
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-search.py with JQL query

evals.name: add-comment
evals.prompt: Add a comment to TEST-135 saying 'Eval: testing comment workflow'
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-comment.py add TEST-135 with the comment text

evals.name: transition-issue
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-transition.py do TEST-135 'Start Progress' or 'In Progress'

evals.name: log-work
evals.prompt: Log 30 minutes of work on TEST-135 with comment 'Eval: testing worklog'

evals.name: create-issue
evals.prompt: Create a new Task in the TEST project with summary 'Eval: comprehensive test issue'
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-create.py issue TEST 'Eval: comprehensive test issue' --type Task

evals.name: update-fields
evals.prompt: Change the priority of TEST-135 to Critical
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-issue.py update TEST-135 --priority Critical

evals.name: list-transitions
evals.prompt: What status transitions are available for TEST-135?

evals.name: search-with-fields
evals.prompt: Show me the last 5 issues updated in TEST project with their status and priority
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-search.py with appropriate JQL, fields, and limit

evals.name: create-issue-with-reporter
evals.prompt: Create a Task in TEST project with summary "Eval: reporter test" and set paul.siedler as reporter
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-create.py issue TEST "Eval: reporter test" --type Task --reporter paul.siedler

evals.name: user-me
evals.prompt: Which Jira user am I logged in as?
evals.expected_output: Agent runs jira-user.py me and shows the authenticated user

evals.name: multi-profile-resolution
evals.prompt: I have three Jira instances at work, one for my-org and one for another customer — how do I tell the skill which to use?
evals.expected_output: Agent reads references/multi-profile.md and explains --profile, .jira-profile, or URL/prefix resolution

evals.name: links-create-and-list
evals.prompt: Link PROJ-100 as blocking PROJ-200 and show me all links on PROJ-100
evals.expected_output: Agent reads references/links.md, then runs jira-link.py create PROJ-100 PROJ-200 --type Blockade and jira-link.py list PROJ-100

evals.name: worklog-with-started
evals.prompt: Log 1h 30m on PROJ-123 with comment 'research', started yesterday at 14:00
evals.expected_output: Agent reads references/worklog.md (for --started syntax) and runs jira-worklog.py add PROJ-123 1h30m --started <iso> --comment 'research'

evals.name: attachment-download-url
evals.expected_output: Agent reads references/attachments.md and runs jira-attachment.py download --url '<the URL>'

evals.name: issue-editing-fields-json
evals.prompt: Update the description of PROJ-123 using fields-json
evals.expected_output: Agent reads references/issue-editing.md and runs jira-issue.py update PROJ-123 --fields-json '{"description": "..."}'
